title: SVG.js: A Lightweight JSON-Driven SVG Icon Rendering Engine for Resource-Constrained Web Environments
description:
SVG.js is a zero-dependency, client-side SVG icon rendering engine that maps icon identifiers to inline SVG paths through a flat JSON dictionary. All icons are defined as path strings inside a single JavaScript object (svg.path), injected into the DOM via a two-method API: svg.icon() for programmatic string generation and svg.di() for automatic class-based DOM injection using the di-{key} naming convention. The engine eliminates external icon font dependencies and HTTP round-trips, making it suitable for low-bandwidth educational web environments in Indonesia. Theming is fully controlled through CSS custom properties (--pColor, --sColor, --aColor), enabling seamless integration with institutional branding systems. SVG.js ships with an interactive gallery (index.html) supporting live search, four display modes (thumbnail, list, 1×, 2×), and one-click clipboard copy of markup snippets. The library provides 60+ icon paths across UI primitives, social brand identifiers, action controls, and ecosystem identity marks for the DonatJS framework family. All paths conform to a 24×24 viewBox coordinate space. SVG.js functions as the visual language layer (cenil) within the DonatJS ecosystem architecture: donat (structure) → wafel (appearance) → cenil (visual language) → piawai (learning). Architecturally, SVG.js is the fourth generation in the Ktupad software lineage: Ktupad (EC00201952487, 2019, PHP MVC) → BayamJS (EC00202367008, 2023, JavaScript MVC) → DonatJS (EC00202414144, 2024, JSON-driven MVC) → SVG.js (2024, JSON-driven SVG engine).

This work is grounded in peer-reviewed research on SVG rendering performance, web accessibility, front-end optimization, and technology adoption in resource-constrained educational environments. Key references: (1) Schwab et al. (2022) demonstrate that SVG — as the dominant markup language for web visualizations — is open, accessible, and customizable via CSS, and fully searchable via the DOM (DOI: 10.1109/TVCG.2021.3059294). (2) Zhang et al. (2023) address automated accessibility optimization for web-based SVG buttons, directly motivating the di-{key} injection pattern (DOI: 10.1016/j.eswa.2023.120836). (3) Riet et al. (2023) provide industrial evidence that reducing HTTP round-trips and front-end asset complexity yields measurable performance gains (DOI: 10.1016/j.jss.2022.111593). (4) Ekpobimi et al. (2024) frame the challenge of front-end performance in heterogeneous device and network environments (DOI: 10.58175/gjarr.2024.2.1.0032). (5) Okai-Ugbaje et al. (2022) establish a framework for mobile and web learning tools in resource-constrained higher education contexts (DOI: 10.1007/s10639-022-11094-5). (6) Kynge (2020) identifies the need for lightweight, offline-capable, locally relevant web content in low-resource educational settings (DOI: 10.2196/16946). (7) Madni et al. (2022) document infrastructure and adoption barriers for e-learning technologies in developing countries including Indonesia (DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2022.915596).

references: Schwab, M., Saffo, D., Bond, N., Sinha, S., Dunne, C., Huang, J., Tompkin, J., & Borkin, M. A. (2022). DOI: 10.1109/TVCG.2021.3059294

Riet, J., Malavolta, I., & Ghaleb, T. (2023). Optimise along the way: An industrial case study on web performance. Journal of Systems and Software, 198, 111593. DOI: 10.1016/j.jss.2022.111593

notes: Intellectual Property context: SVG.js is part of the DonatJS ecosystem (EC00202414144), registered at the Direktorat Jenderal Kekayaan Intelektual (DJKI), Republic of Indonesia. Foundational lineage includes Ktupad Core (EC00201952487) and BayamJS (EC00202367008). Related books: 'Pengembangan Aplikasi Web Modern dengan DonatJS' (P4I, 2024, ISBN 978-623-848-897-1); 'Optimasi Penggunaan SVG dalam Web Development' (P4I, 2025, ISBN 978-623-876-765-6). SINTA Author ID: 6848496. Patent application: S00202503129 (DJKI).
